zh.lifeSummary: 當Dulcia Trafford於1417年出生在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，她的父親Edmund de Trafford I27歲，母親Alice de Venables19歲。她於1438年與John Beauford在英國英格蘭藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1474年4月4日在英國爾迪斯利享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Trafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Trafford (Lancashire), Trafford in Chipping Warden (Northamptonshire), and Bridge, Mickle, and Wimbolds Trafford (Cheshire). The Northamptonshire placename probably derives from Old English træppe ‘trap’ + ford ‘ford’. The Cheshire placenames derive from Old English trōg ‘trough’ + ford. The Lancashire placename is a development of Stretford (Lancashire); most cases of the surname probably derive from this place. Early members of the de Trafford family, recorded here from the 13th century, were also recorded in medieval documents as de Stratford(e). Both the surname and the placename lost the initial S-. The manor of Trafford was carved out of Stretford township, and perhaps because its manor house was located at some distance from Stretford village, Trafford was adopted for the new settlement from an early date. See also Stratford .

variants.name: Stratford
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Stratford, for example in Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Middlesex, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and Wiltshire, from Old English strǣt ‘Roman road’ (see Street ) + ford ‘ford’.

habitational name principally from Strafford Sands (now lost) in Mexborough (Yorkshire), presumed to be the ancient meeting-place of Strafforth Wapentake, and deriving from the same source as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Dulcia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Learned re-creation in the 19th century of the medieval name Dowse, Late Latin Dulcia, a derivative of dulcis ‘sweet’. It is borne by the film and stage actress Dulcie Gray ( 1919–2011 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Dulcia Trafford was born in 1417, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, her father, Edmund de Trafford I, was 27 and her mother, Alice de Venables, was 19. She married John Beauford in 1438, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 4 April 1474, in Disley, Cheshire, England, at the age of 57.

ko.Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈턴 운더 리니
ko.lifeSummary: Dulcia Trafford(이)가 1417년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund de Trafford I(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Venables(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1438년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 John Beauford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1474년 4월 4일 영국 체실르 디슬리에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England: аштон ундир лин, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dulcia Trafford родилась в 1417 г. в местности аштон-ундир-лин, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund de Trafford I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Venables, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Beauford в 1438 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 4 апреля 1474 г. в местности дисли, чешир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.
